check_content_excerpt:
Verify that Advanced Intrusion Detection Environment (AIDE) is properly configured to use cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of audit tools with the following command: 
 
$ egrep '(\/sbin\/(audit|au))' /etc/aide/aide.conf
/sbin/auditctl p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/auditd p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/ausearch p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/aureport p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/autrace p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/augenrules p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
 
If any of the seven audit tools do not have appropriate selection lines, this is a finding.
fix_text_excerpt:
Add or update the following selection lines for "/etc/aide/aide.conf" to protect the integrity of the audit tools: 
 
# Audit Tools 
/sbin/auditctl p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/auditd p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/ausearch p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/aureport p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/autrace p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512 
/sbin/augenrules p+i+n+u+g+s+b+acl+xattrs+sha512
title: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS must use cryptographic mechanisms to protect the integrity of audit tools.
